creator: rs@bytecamp.net
text:
(In reply to Armin Gruner from comment #17)

The behaviour you see is documented:

rpcbind(8)
> Note that when specifying IP addresses with -h, rpcbind will automatically add 
> 127.0.0.1 and if IPv6 is enabled, ::1 to the list.

The actual topic of this PR is different: rpcbind binds to 0.0.0.0 even when a specific set of addresses is given as argument to -h. 

0.0.0.0 means ANY address, this must not be confused with the mapping that occurs for 127.0.0.1 (-> primary jail ip) in a jailed environment.

regards,
Robert Schulze
